summary: Absent collab ledgers are handled gracefully by the monitor and explicitly required by protocol; launcher pre-creates protocol + ledger files in metaswarm runs, so absence is unlikely at start but still possible later (e.g., deletion/race).

findings.issue: Protocol explicitly requires reader tolerance for absent ledger files
findings.details: The collaboration protocol states readers must tolerate absent files and malformed trailing lines.

findings.issue: Monitor consumer degrades safely when ledgers are missing
findings.details: count_jsonl_lines returns 0 when a ledger file is absent, and collab summary uses this function for all ledger counters.

findings.issue: Launcher initialization makes missing ledgers unlikely at run start
findings.details: In metaswarm mode launcher creates collab dir, touches each JSONL ledger via append-open, and writes protocol.json atomically before agent work.

findings.issue: Absence remains possible in practice after initialization
findings.details: Commander flow depends on consuming/writing collab ledgers during runtime; primary targets show startup creation and monitor fallback, but no additional runtime hardening in these targets against post-start file removal.
